steps.title: Match the antecedent
steps.content: The blank refers back to the human brain, the subject introduced at the start of the sentence. Since brain is singular and inanimate, the pronoun must also be singular and neuter.

steps.title: Reject mismatched pronouns
steps.content: Choice A is a feminine pronoun for a person or animal, not an inanimate noun. Choices B and D are plural, but the antecedent is singular. The singular neuter pronoun fits the human brain. The answer is C.
